# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    # Using ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic, ie filter by category and price range when searching for products
     @products = Product.where("name LIKE ?", "%#{params[:search][:query]}%").where(category_id: params[:search][:categories]) if !params[:search].blank? &&  (checked=Array.wrap(params[:search][:price]))  
    else [] end     # default is to show all products when no search parameter or price range selected at the same time     
       @products ||= Product.all        # Default case, ie., if there are not searched items and a user didn't select any criteria then it will return all product entries  (for security purpose)     end   self   `end def products_params
!ruby#971@ProductsController
